pages.raw_text:
The discharge Of two transfers
yesterday who are members of the
International Transfers union No.
I202, who were employed on city
work, has created quite a stir in
labor circles. The men claim, as
does the business agent of the
union, that the orders for their dis
charge were given by Consulting
Engineer Gill because they were not
members Of local Transfers union
No. 101.


The incident revives to certaia
extent the fight between the two
teamsters' unions. Transfers
union No. 101 has been employed
by the city on all public works and
the International Transfers have
been trying to secure portion of
such work. but without success.
Several times, It Is stated. Inter
national Transfers hive worked on
city work, only to be run out by
